mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side weakness that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of instances when INDPRO drops below its 12-month MA during post-shock recovery phases, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation compresses real discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines and CPIAUCSL MoM change < 0.3%
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth (Rank IC < 0.03) over rolling 3-year windows, or returns are negative in > 55% of expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP signals an economic contraction that immediately triggers equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, or the signal fails to predict direction (accuracy < 55%) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside expanding retail sales confirms demand-pull supply utilization that boosts corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both positive MoM
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances during mid-cycle expansion, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.
